title: A Trip to the Moon: Following Astronauts on a Big Adventure
summary: This article is about a reporter who followed a space mission called Artemis II for 10 days. Four astronauts, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen, traveled to the Moon and back in a rocket. The reporter watched from Florida as the rocket launched with a bright light and loud noise. The astronauts lived in a small capsule like a minibus, with no privacy, and used a special toilet that cost a lot of money. They saw Earth from space and said it looked beautiful. The mission was exciting and historic because humans went further into space than ever before.

keywords.explanation: a big machine that goes up into space
the big round object in the night sky
the small room where astronauts live in space

How long did the mission last?

background_read: Space missions like Artemis II help us learn about the Moon and space. Astronauts train for years to go on these trips. They use rockets to leave Earth and live in small capsules because space is very different from home. Missions like this are important for science and exploring new places.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts (Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Jeremy Hansen) and a reporter
WHAT: A historic mission to the Moon and back
WHERE: Kennedy Space Center in Florida and space
WHY: To explore space and go further than humans have before

perspectives.description: The reporter felt very excited and shared the thrilling experience of watching the launch and following the mission.
